context: =  group of heterogeneous disorders caused by interstitial deposits of an insoluble fibrillar protein-polysaccharide in various organs impairing tissue function → hypoxia, mucosal edema, hemorrhage, ulceration, mucosal atrophy, muscle atrophy Cause: (a)   prolonged antigenic stimulation of RES by chronic infection (b)  disorder of immunoincompetence (c)  aging (d)  idiopathic Histo:   amorphous eosinophilic hyaline material deposited around terminal blood vessels, stains with Congo red + crystal violet; green birefringence under polarizing light; amyloid fibrils have β-pleated sheet structure
